data.title: Bitcoin News Today: Bitcoin Breaks Above $78K as Iran De-Escalation Drives Oil Lower
data.description:
Key Takeaways
Bitcoin surged above $78,000 as geopolitical tensions eased.Iran confirmed the Strait of Hormuz would remain open during the ceasefire period.Oil prices plunged nearly 10%, reducing inflation concerns.Bitcoin has now decisively broken through the key $76,000–$77,000 resistance zone.The next major upside targets are $80,000 and potentially $82,000–$85,000.
Bitcoin Pushes Above $78,000
Bitcoin climbed above $78,000 after Iran announced that the Strait of Hormuz would remain fully open for commercial shipping during the current ceasefire period.
BTC rose to around $78,100, extending its breakout after several failed attempts to reclaim the $76,000–$77,000 area over recent weeks. The move marks Bitcoin’s strongest price level since early February.
Oil Prices Crash as Hormuz Reopens
The market reaction was driven by comments from Iranian Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi, who said the Strait of Hormuz would remain fully open for all commercial vessels for the duration of the ceasefire.
The announcement triggered a sharp decline in oil prices:
West Texas Intermediate fell nearly 10% toward the low-$80 range.Brent Crude dropped toward the high-$80 range.
Lower oil prices reduce concerns about inflation, which had become a major macro headwind for both crypto and equities in recent weeks.
Risk Assets Rally Across Markets
The easing of tensions in the Middle East boosted risk appetite globally.
US stock index futures moved higher, bond yields eased, and crypto markets rallied broadly. Investors interpreted the Hormuz reopening as a sign that the risk of a major energy shock may be fading.
Bitcoin Breakout Signals Stronger Momentum
The $76,000–$77,000 zone had been one of Bitcoin’s most important resistance levels.
BTC repeatedly failed near this area in February and March after the sharp correction toward $60,000. Now that Bitcoin has moved decisively above that range, traders are increasingly looking toward:
$80,000 as the next psychological resistance$82,000–$85,000 as the next major supply zonePotential short-covering and momentum buying if BTC holds above breakout levels
The breakout also puts Bitcoin firmly above its 100-day moving average, strengthening the bullish technical outlook.
Broader Iran Talks Continue
Separate reports suggest the US and Iran are discussing a broader framework to end hostilities.
Negotiations may include:
Release of frozen Iranian assetsLimits on Iran’s enriched uranium stockpileLonger-term guarantees around shipping access through the Strait of Hormuz
If progress continues, the combination of lower oil prices and easing geopolitical tensions could remain supportive for Bitcoin and other risk assets in the near term.

data.title: Trump: Iran agreed to "everything"
data.description: Trump told NewsNation that Iran had agreed to "everything," including stopping uranium enrichment, agreeing not to close the Strait of Hormuz, and that it would no longer be used as a weapon against the world. According to an Axios reporter, the US and Iran have made progress in negotiations on a three-page memorandum of understanding to end the war. (Jinshi)

data.title: Trump Announces Iran's Agreement on Key Issues
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump stated on April 17 that Iran has agreed to several significant terms, according to BlockBeats. Iran has consented to keep the Strait of Hormuz open, ensuring it will not be used as a tool against the global community. Additionally, Iran has agreed to halt uranium enrichment.

Following Trump's remarks regarding the Strait of Hormuz, crude oil prices fell over 12%, and European natural gas prices experienced a temporary 10% drop.

data.title: Strategy Shares Surge Over 12% Amid Bitcoin Rally
data.description: Strategy shares experienced a significant increase, rising approximately 12.33% to trade at $167.44. According to NS3.AI, this surge occurred alongside a broader rally in Bitcoin, which has positively impacted related stocks.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 78,000 USDT with a 5.61% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 17, 2026, 14:43 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 78,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 78,026.3125 USDT, with a narrowed 5.61% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: AI Company DeepSeek Pursues Initial Funding with Valuation Over $10 Billion
data.description: AI company DeepSeek is currently in the process of securing its first round of funding, with a valuation exceeding $10 billion. According to Foresight News, this development was reported by The Information. The substantial valuation highlights the growing interest and investment in artificial intelligence technologies.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Announces Iran's Removal of Naval Mines
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has announced that Iran, with assistance from the United States, has removed or is in the process of removing all naval mines. According to Odaily, this development marks a significant step in maritime safety and security. The removal of these mines is expected to reduce tensions and enhance navigation safety in the region.

data.title: Crypto wallet provider Exodus partners with Ripple to expand support for XRPL and RLUSD
data.description: Exodus Movement, a US-listed cryptocurrency wallet provider and Bitcoin treasury company, announced a partnership with Ripple to expand native support for XRP Ledger (XRPL) in its wallets. It also added in-wallet support for the stablecoin Ripple USD (RLUSD), allowing users to directly manage and send XRP assets within their wallets. (Globenewswire)

data.title: Financial compliance startup Spektr raises $20 million in Series A funding, led by NEA.
data.description: Financial compliance startup Spektr announced the completion of a $20 million Series A funding round, led by New Enterprise Associates (NEA), with participation from Northzone, Seedcamp, and PSV Tech. This brings its total funding to $26 million. The company primarily provides AI-powered compliance services, including risk assessment and sanctions list monitoring, to crypto wallet service providers such as Phantom and traditional financial companies. (Crunchbase)

data.title: Market Rebound Impacts Crypto Short Positions
data.description: A recent market rebound has significantly affected the short positions of the wallet known as '聪 pension-usdt.eth.' According to Foresight News, the wallet's short positions on 1,000 BTC and 20,000 ETH have resulted in unrealized losses exceeding $15.5 million. Consequently, the total profit of the wallet has decreased from $33.28 million to $14.98 million. Previously, this address had achieved a winning streak of 20 consecutive trades, boasting a success rate of over 85%.

data.title: Neo Co-Founder Proposes Governance Overhaul Amid Asset Disclosure
data.description: Neo co-founder Da Hongfei has suggested a comprehensive reset of the treasury and governance structure following a recent disclosure revealing approximately $461 million in assets held by the Neo Foundation and Neo Global Development as of the end of 2025. According to NS3.AI, the proposed plan includes relocating the foundation to the Cayman Islands, establishing a five-member board along with an independent Supervisor, and prohibiting either founder from participating in these bodies for a period of 24 months. However, Erik Zhang has expressed opposition to the proposal, arguing that it continues to depend excessively on offchain structures and lacks transparency in attestations.

data.title: Trump: Iran, with U.S. assistance, has removed or is removing all its sea mines.
data.description: US President Trump stated that Iran, with US assistance, has removed or is in the process of removing all its sea mines. (Jinshi)

data.title: Crypto Executives Boost Security Amid Rising Kidnap Threats
data.description: Crypto executives are ramping up their personal security measures in response to a growing number of kidnap and ransom incidents. According to NS3.AI, Jameson Lopp's tracker has recorded at least 316 such cases since 2014. The repository indicates 79 attacks occurred in 2025, with 27 media-reported incidents targeting crypto holders already in 2026. In response to these threats, France introduced a prevention platform during Paris Blockchain Week. Additionally, insurers have noted a significant increase in demand for kidnap and ransom coverage.

data.title: Trump Criticizes NATO's Role in Hormuz Strait Tensions
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump has commented on the situation in the Hormuz Strait, stating that the current tensions have largely subsided. According to BlockBeats, Trump revealed that NATO had contacted him to offer assistance, which he declined.

Trump explicitly told NATO not to intervene unless they intended to take advantage of the situation to fill their ships with oil. He criticized NATO's performance during the crisis, describing it as ineffective and labeling the alliance a 'paper tiger.'

These remarks highlight the differences between the United States and its NATO allies regarding Middle Eastern security issues and underscore the uncertainty in coordination within the Western bloc amid the current geopolitical climate.

data.title: Apple Sees Growth in iPhone Shipments to China and India
data.description: iPhone shipments to China increased by 20% in the first quarter of 2026, according to Counterpoint Research. The report also highlighted a rise in iPhone consumption in India, reaching 9% in 2026. Additionally, Knockout Stocks has issued a bullish price target for AAPL, reflecting positive market sentiment.

data.title: Brent Crude Drops as Iran Reopens Strait of Hormuz During Ceasefire
data.description: Iran has reopened the Strait of Hormuz for commercial vessels amid a ceasefire, leading to a significant drop in Brent crude prices by 6.84% to approximately $92. According to NS3.AI, access to the strait is restricted to routes coordinated by Iran’s Ports and Maritime Organisation and is valid only for the duration of the truce. Concurrently, U.S. crude prices decreased by 7.04%, while Bitcoin experienced a rise of 1.59%, and the S&P 500 saw a slight increase of 0.07%. The current US-Iran ceasefire is set to expire on April 21, and any breach of the Lebanon truce could result in the closure of the strait once again.

data.title: Trump: The United States will acquire all nuclear materials and prohibit Israel from bombing Lebanon again.
data.description: US President Trump posted on social media, stating, "The United States will receive all the nuclear 'dust' (i.e., nuclear material) produced by our great B-2 bombers—without any monetary transaction in any form or manner. This deal is also unrelated to Lebanon, but the United States will work alone with Lebanon to properly handle the Hezbollah situation. Israel will no longer bomb Lebanon; they are prohibited from doing so by the United States." Trump used the term "nuclear dust" to refer to highly enriched uranium. Trump's statement praised the United States for destroying Iran's nuclear program through its powerful military force (B-2 bombers). It is believed that this highly enriched uranium was buried deep underground after the US military strikes on the Fordow and Natanz nuclear facilities last June. This nuclear material has remained in Iranian control and has consistently been a significant source of tension between the Trump administration and Tehran. (Jinshi)

data.title: Polish Prime Minister Accuses Zondacrypto of Political Influence and Russian Ties
data.description: Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk has accused Zondacrypto of financially supporting political opponents and attempting to influence Poland's cryptocurrency legislation. According to NS3.AI, Tusk claims that the company is connected to funds associated with the Russian mafia and may have links to Russian intelligence agencies. The controversy revolves around a legislative bill intended to align Poland with the European Union's Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) framework. This bill has faced significant hurdles, including a presidential veto and a failed attempt to override the veto in December.

data.title: Brother Machi's long positions have already yielded a floating profit of $2.597 million, of which the ETH long positions alone have a floating profit of $1.947 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, Brother Machi's long positions have already yielded a floating profit of $2.597 million. His total long positions in BTC, ETH, and HYPE, worth $58.78 million, are all currently showing a profit, with the ETH long position alone showing a floating profit of $1.947 million. Brother Machi has also set profit-taking points for some of his positions: ▶︎ BTC: $77,000 ▶︎ ETH: $2,420 - $2,450 ▶︎ HYPE: $4.888 - $45.5

data.title: Analysis: Iran announces Hormuz is "fully open," but industry stakeholders remain cautious.
data.description: Iran announced that the Strait of Hormuz would be open for the remainder of the ceasefire, but industry stakeholders remain cautious. A senior Iranian military official stated that merchant ships could pass through the Strait of Hormuz along designated routes with permission from the Revolutionary Guard Navy. German shipping company Hapag-Lloyd stated that it would temporarily refrain from transiting the strait while assessing the announcement. A spokesperson for the company stated in an emailed statement: “We are now beginning to assess the new situation and the risks involved. Therefore, we will remain temporarily refraining from transiting the strait.” The CEO of the Norwegian Shipowners' Association stated, “The situation in the Strait of Hormuz remains unresolved, with several unresolved uncertainties, including the presence of mines, Iran's applicable conditions, and issues related to actual implementation. These aspects need to be clarified before assessing any transit.” The President of Finland welcomed Iran's announcement regarding the opening of the Strait of Hormuz. However, a lasting solution requires diplomatic means. The Swedish Prime Minister stated that Sweden is prepared to ensure safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz after a permanent ceasefire is achieved. EU High Representative for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy Karas stated that the Strait of Hormuz must remain open and free of charge. Iran must abandon any plans to impose transit fees. (Jinshi)
